bids.reasoning: This is the opening at t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages to infer their preferences, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. With no time pressure yet, I should anchor strongly on my best values across all issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent’s only offer is the minimum-utility bid for me on every issue, so there is no evidence of meaningful concession yet. I am targeting a very high utility while avoiding an exact repeat of my opening bid, conceding only on my lowest-weight issueA and holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04), so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has moved only slightly, from all valueA to improving issueD to valueB, which raises my utility but still remains far from acceptable; I will concede further only on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (~0.952) because it is still extremely early at t=0.06, so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has moved from all valueA to conceding strongly on issueC and issueD, and since issueA is my lowest-weight issue, I will make a small further concession there while holding firm on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid of about 0.935, conceding only on the lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and especially D. Time is still very early at t=0.08, and the opponent has shown some movement toward my preferences, including a strong concession at t=0.06, though their latest offer pulled back slightly. With no messages to consider, I will continue gradual concession without giving up high-weight issues.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early, so I should concede only modestly while preserving my highest-weight issues. The opponent has consistently pushed issueA low while recently improving issueC and issueD substantially, so I concede the least important issueA to valueA but hold issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.89 because it is still early at t=0.12, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me, especially on issueC and issueD, while they seem resistant on issueB; I therefore concede modestly on the lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.856, which is a modest concession from my earlier high-utility demands while still protecting my most important issues, especially issueD and issueC. Since it is still early at t=0.14, I do not need to drop to the opponent’s latest bid, though their offers show real concession on issueC and issueD while holding tougher positions on issueA and issueB. With no messages to evaluate, I am relying on the offer pattern and conceding first on lower-weight issues.
